chunks.text: In 1962, the American marine biologist Rachel Carson published a book called Silent Spring.
It was a book that warned how much the use of chemical pesticide was destroying nature.
At the time, DDT was being used in great amounts to kill insects.
But pesticide did not kill only insects.
and human beings as well were being harmed by the poison that accumulated in their bodies over time.
" Spring may come, and yet no bird may sing.
" Carson's words awakened a great awareness in the people.
The chemical industry attacked her violently and criticized her work.
But her warning was eventually accepted, and the United States banned the use of DDT.
Carson's book became the starting point of the modern environmental movement.
To preserve the ecological balance is also to preserve humanity itself.
